heading: Medal of honor; distinguished-service cross; distinguished-service medal: delegation of power to award
label: 7275 Medal of honor; distinguished-service cross; distinguished-service medal: delegation of power to award
text: The President may delegate his authority to award the medal of honor, distinguished-service cross, and distinguished-service medal, to a commanding general of a separate army or higher unit in the field.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)374510:1410.(16th par., less words after semicolon, under “Ordnance Department”), .July 9, 1918, ch. 14340 Stat. 872The words “under such conditions, regulations, and limitations as he shall prescribe” are omitted as surplusage. The words “his authority” are substituted for the words “the power conferred upon him by sections 1403, 1406–1408, 1409–1412, 1416, 1420, 1422, 1423, and 1424 of this title”.
Amendments2018— renumbered as this section.Pub. L. 115–232section 3745 of this title

Effective Date of 2018 AmendmentAmendment by effective , with provision for the coordination of amendments and special rule for certain redesignations, see , set out as a note preceding .Pub. L. 115–232section 800 of Pub. L. 115–232section 3001 of this titleFeb. 1, 2019
